rationale.R1.1: A review of this section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent and appropriately stated.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation centrally implies checking whether the argument moves consistently from problem motivation to method claim, related work contrast, and empirical positioning.
rationale.R1.3: The review should assess whether the reported benchmark results support the claims made about calibration and practical usefulness.
rationale.R1.4: Because the section makes a strong first-procedure claim and contrasts against prior CATE inference work, novelty assessment is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.5: Reviewing a section includes evaluating whether the ordering of motivation, contribution, related work, contrast, and evaluation creates an effective narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level assessment of clarity, register, concision, and flow.
rationale.R1.7: The section relies on named prior work and attribution, so citation coverage and reference use are central to the review responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended without overstating specificity.
rationale.RX.2: The broad review request requires guarding against inflated judgments about novelty, calibration guarantees, and default-use claims.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting requested responsibilities rather than performing the review or revising the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Review responsibilities include sensitivity to downstream harms from unsupported causal-inference claims or misleading uncertainty guarantees.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact, such as the contribution claim, related work, and reported results.
